drop_zone.title: Drop or Select Files
drop_zone.subtitle: Multiple files supported, direct to peer.
drop_zone.overlay_subtitle: Release to start sending your files instantly

sender.ready_desc: Generate a secure, single-use code for instant P2P transfer.
sender.waiting: Waiting for a partner to enter the access key.
sender.expired_desc: The access key expired for security. Click above to generate a new one.

receiver.hint: Keys are generated by the host and expire after use.

connected.drag_and_drop_hint: Files you select or receive will appear here instantly.

dialog.transfer_active: Active transfers in progress. Severing connection will abort all ongoing data streams. Proceed?
dialog.switch_mode: Switching modes will terminate current transfers. Continue?

toast.partner_disconnected: Partner has disconnected. You can still access the files in progress.
toast.receive_error: Error receiving data. Please try again.
toast.connection_issue: Connection issue encountered. Please try again.
toast.handshake_error: Error during handshake. Please try again.
toast.create_session_error: Could not create a new session.
toast.join_error: Could not join connection. Please check the code.
toast.integrity_error: Data integrity check failed. The file may be corrupted.

error.title: Connection Failed
error.network_issue: Could not establish a peer-to-peer connection. This is often due to network restrictions (like corporate firewalls) or one side losing internet connection.
error.network_restricted: Connection blocked by network. Your network environment (Firewall, NAT) does not allow direct P2P connections.
error.invalid_code: Invalid access key or expired. Please verify the code from the sending device.
error.offline: You are currently offline. Please check your internet connection and try again.
error.troubleshoot_1: Ensure both devices have a stable internet connection.
error.troubleshoot_2: Try switching to a different network (e.g., use 4G/5G instead of corporate Wi-Fi).
error.troubleshoot_3: Disable VPN, Proxy, or firewall software if you are using one.

footer.description: Uses ephemeral signaling for private handshake.

guide.title: User Guide
guide.step_1_desc: The sender selects 'Generate access key'. The receiver selects 'Enter code'.
guide.step_2_desc: Enter the 8-character code from the sender into the receiver and click 'Establish Connection'.
guide.step_3_desc: Once connected, you can drag and drop or select files to send. Files are transferred directly between devices.
guide.p2p_note: Note: Sendly uses WebRTC for direct data transfer. Your data does not pass through any server, ensuring maximum speed and absolute privacy.
